rationale:
Upper band = 4× APRA system-wide commercial NPL. Reasonable cap
for a non-bank SME term loan book given non-bank-vs-bank
selection differential. Lower band = 0.5× system NPL (anything
below this is unrealistic for non-bank lending).
Revolving facility characteristics warrant slightly higher upper\nband than term lending (~25% uplift to account for utilisation\ndrift and faster delinquency emergence).
Short-tenor product with security in receivables; shorter\nobservation cycle but lower PD per period.
Big 4 corporate Pillar 3 × 2. Trade finance peer class is closer\nto corporate-general than to SME.

Pepper Money's asset finance loss expense ~3.5% in 2024 (with
late-stage arrears uplift). Upper band ~25% above as buffer.
Source ID will switch to PEPPER_ASSET_FINANCE_LOSS_EXPENSE_2024
when the Pepper adapter parses real disclosures.

La Trobe published realised bridging loss is 0.8% of book.
Implied default rate ≈ 0.8% / ~30% LGD = ~2.7%; upper band
≈ 2× this gives 5–6%, rounded up to 8% accounting for
definition-alignment uncertainty. APS 113 Satisfactory PD
= 2.4% provides a cross-check.

APS 113 specialised lending slotting grades directly bracket
the realistic range for development PD. Strong=0.4%, Good=0.8%,
Satisfactory=2.4%, Weak=5%. LRA below Good or above Weak warrants
review.
APRA QPEX system-wide CRE impaired ratio is the canonical\nbenchmark. Upper band = 2.5× this allows for non-bank book\ncomposition; lower band = 0.5× catches under-conservatism.
Prime residential RMBS 30+ DPD arrears band for future bridging\nLGD and stress-testing reality checks. Not consumed by the\ncommercial-lending PD workbook.
Non-conforming residential RMBS 30+ DPD arrears band for future\nbridging LGD and stress-testing reality checks. Not consumed by\nthe commercial-lending PD workbook.
